```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
mu0   <- 5
mu1   <- sample(c(7, 8, 9), 1)
sigma <- 2
n     <- sample(c(25, 36, 49), 1)
alpha <- 0.05

## Valor critico e poder
c_val <- round(mu0 + qnorm(1 - alpha) * sigma / sqrt(n), 4)
poder <- round(1 - pnorm((c_val - mu1) / (sigma / sqrt(n))), 4)

## Respostas
correct <- poder
w1 <- round(1 - poder, 4)
w2 <- round(poder - 0.1, 4)
w3 <- round(pnorm((c_val - mu0) / (sigma / sqrt(n))), 4)
w4 <- round(0.05, 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
UMP de Karlin-Rubin para $H_0:\mu \leq `r mu0`$ vs. $H_1:\mu > `r mu0`$, com $\sigma = `r sigma`$ e $n = `r n`$. O valor critico e $\bar{X} > `r c_val`$. O poder quando $\mu = `r mu1`$ e:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
Poder $= P(\bar{X} > `r c_val` \mid \mu = `r mu1`) = 1 - \Phi\!\left(\frac{`r c_val` - `r mu1`}{`r sigma`/\sqrt{`r n`}}\right) = 1 - \Phi(`r round((c_val-mu1)/(sigma/sqrt(n)),4)`) = `r poder`$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap6S4Q7_KR_poder_normal
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
